tldr: Move from Iconify's universal multi-set runtime to Phosphor's curated 1200+ icon family with six weight variants and tree-shakeable framework packages.
why_switch: Single curated visual language instead of mixing styles across many sets
Six built-in weights (thin, light, regular, bold, fill, duotone) for expressive UI
Tree-shakeable per-framework packages avoid runtime icon lookups
MIT-licensed across the whole family with predictable maintenance
Simpler mental model: one library, one API, one set of names
what_to_export: List of every Iconify icon name in use (e.g. mdi:home, ph:gear)
Icon size, color, and stroke conventions in your design system
Wrapper components or hooks that resolve Iconify names
Accessibility props (aria-label, role) attached to icons
Bundling/SSR config related to Iconify on-demand loading
Figma icon library mappings or design tokens
Locations where icons are referenced via raw SVG dump
Any custom icon sets you registered with Iconify
export_method: Grep your codebase for Iconify imports and the Iconify component usages to enumerate every icon name. Group names by the source set they came from (e.g. material-symbols, heroicons) and produce a CSV mapping. Iconify icon names live as strings, so a search across .tsx/.vue/.svelte/.html files is usually sufficient. Consult the official Iconify docs for any project-specific export tooling before relying on assumptions.
import_method: Install the Phosphor package for your framework (e.g. @phosphor-icons/react, @phosphor-icons/vue) per its official docs. Replace Iconify component calls with named Phosphor imports, choosing the desired weight variant. Update your wrapper component (if any) so consumers pass a Phosphor component instead of a string name. Verify per-icon imports so the bundler can tree-shake.

concept_mapping.note: Compile-time import vs runtime string lookup.
Pick the closest Phosphor equivalent; some icons may not exist 1:1.
Bundler tree-shakes unused icons; no network fetch.
Phosphor exposes weight variants instead of swapping sets.
Re-link design components; consult Phosphor docs for current Figma assets.
Phosphor does not aggregate third-party sets; ship your own SVGs separately.

gotchas: Not every Iconify icon has a Phosphor equivalent; budget time for substitutes
Importing all weights for the same icon balloons bundle size — pick one default weight
SSR/hydration code paths that relied on Iconify's runtime fetch will need removal
Naming differs (kebab-case strings vs PascalCase components); a manual mapping table helps
Accessibility attributes must be re-added per icon; defaults differ between libraries
If you used niche sets (e.g. brand logos) via Iconify, you may need a separate source

email.subject: Switching our icons from Iconify to Phosphor
email.body:
Hey team,

I'm planning to migrate our icon usage from Iconify to Phosphor Icons. Phosphor gives us a single curated family with six weights, tree-shakeable framework packages, and an MIT license — which simplifies our design system and trims runtime overhead.

First step: I'll grep the codebase for every Iconify name and produce a mapping CSV to Phosphor equivalents (with notes on icons that don't have a 1:1 match). Then I'll swap imports framework-by-framework and verify bundle size and a11y attributes after each pass. Estimated effort: 4-12 hours depending on coverage.

Let me know if you want to weigh in on the default weight or flag icons we should keep as custom SVGs.

Thanks
